# Retrospective: 2026-02-02

> **Trigger**: Sprint 4 complete (Write Operations), team-requested review after 2 sprints  
> **Participants**: @team-lead, @developer, @tester, @security, @architect  
> **Scope**: Work since last retro (2026-01-31) through Sprint 3 & Sprint 4

## Work Completed Since Last Retro

| Sprint | Focus | Key Deliverables | Tests Added |
|--------|-------|------------------|-------------|
| Sprint 3 | Test Coverage | Custom error handling, provider tests, E2E tests | 164 total |
| Sprint 4 | Write Operations | VaultBackup, SafeVaultAccess write API, EditConfirmationModal | 178 total |

**Releases**: alpha.4 → alpha.5 → alpha.6

---

## What Went Well ✅

| Item | Details |
|------|---------|
| **Action items from last retro** | All 5 items completed and committed (test strategy, TS errors, UX section) |
| **Test-first mindset improving** | Sprint 3 focused purely on coverage, raised from ~30% to 45% |
| **Security gate working** | Sprint 4 write ops had security review task in plan (per @team-lead mandate) |
| **Progressive commits** | 20+ focused conventional commits across both sprints |
| **Safety-first write ops** | Write mode disabled by default, explicit enable required, automatic backups |
| **Coverage trajectory** | 165 → 178 tests, following planned coverage progression |
| **Retro cadence request** | User requested regular retros every 2-3 sprints — good sign of process maturity |

## What Could Improve 🔄

| Item | Root Cause | Impact |
|------|------------|--------|
| **Sprint plan outdated** | Plan showed Sprint 3 as "current" when we were on Sprint 4 | Minor confusion |
| **Coverage target slippage** | alpha.6 planned for 55% but we're likely around 46% | Coverage lagging targets |
| **No exploratory testing on write ops** | Jumped from impl to release without manual testing | Unknown UX gaps |
| **Agent definitions not reviewed** | Last update was at project inception | May not reflect current practices |
| **Engineering Baseline alignment** | Never formally compared against EA-02, EA-03, EA-07 | Potential compliance gaps |

## Engineering Baseline Evaluation

Compared current practices against Ingka Engineering Baseline ADRs:

| ADR | Requirement | Current Status | Gap Analysis |
|-----|-------------|----------------|--------------|
| **EA-02** (Testing & Change Management) | Defined test strategy, traceable changes | ✅ test-strategy.md exists, conventional commits | Consider adding NFR tests |
| **EA-03** (Security & Privacy) | Cyber Journey fundamentals | ⚠️ Security gate exists but no formal threat model | Should document threat model for write ops |
| **EA-07** (Secrets Management) | Secrets via secret manager only | ✅ 1Password integration, no hardcoded secrets | Well aligned |
| **EA-10** (Technical Documentation) | InnerSource guidelines | ⚠️ README exists but no CONTRIBUTING.md | Add contribution guide |

## Agent Definition Updates Needed

Based on this retro and Engineering Baseline review:

| Agent | Recommended Update |
|-------|-------------------|
| `@tester` | Add NFR testing responsibility (EA-02) |
| `@security` | Add threat modelling requirement for write operations (EA-03) |
| `copilot-instructions.md` | Add retro cadence recommendation (every 2-3 sprints) |
| `copilot-instructions.md` | Reference EA-02 test strategy maturity levels |

| Action | Owner | Priority | Status |
|--------|-------|----------|--------|
| Update sprint-plan.md to reflect current state | @team-lead | High | ✅ Done (Sprint 8) |
| Add retro cadence (every 2-3 sprints) to copilot-instructions.md | @team-lead | High | ✅ Done |
| Create threat model for write operations | @security | Medium | ✅ Done — [docs/threat-model.md](/docs/threat-model.md) |
| Add CONTRIBUTING.md for InnerSource readiness (EA-10) | @tech-writer | Medium | ✅ Done — [CONTRIBUTING.md](/CONTRIBUTING.md) |
| Exploratory testing session for write ops UX | @tester | Medium | ✅ Done (informal, Sprint 5) |
| Update @tester agent with NFR testing guidance | @team-lead | Low | ⬜ Deferred to Sprint 10 |

---

## Lessons Learned 💡

1. **Retro cadence matters** — Every 2-3 sprints catches drift before it compounds
2. **Engineering Baseline as reference** — Periodic review against EA standards catches compliance gaps early
3. **Agent definitions age** — Should review agent files when process changes
4. **Coverage targets need adjustment** — Original plan was optimistic; recalibrate to realistic pace
5. **Write operations need exploratory testing** — Automated tests don't catch all UX issues

## Process Changes Proposed

1. **Retro every 2-3 sprints** — Add to copilot-instructions.md as a standard cadence
2. **Baseline review in retros** — Include Engineering Baseline check as standard retro section
3. **Threat model for risky features** — Any feature touching user data needs documented threat model

---

## Next Retro

**Trigger**: After Sprint 6 or 7 (following the 2-3 sprint cadence)  
**Focus**: Progress on action items, write ops user feedback, coverage trajectory
